page: Microsoft Azure D64_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), AES cryptography, and FMA instructions, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, alongside 1638 GB of local storage.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core processing speeds, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, fall into the lower 25th percentile, reflecting the older CPU generation. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, supported by a 50 MB L3 cache and multi-threaded decompression capabilities. Memory bandwidth remains stable in the middle tier, achieving 118.68 GB/sec for large uncached reads and 50.0 GB/sec for writes. Large-scale operations like Redis SET and static web serving also demonstrate average throughput, while LLM text generation performance is poor, indicating a lack of optimization for modern AI inference workloads.

From a resource density perspective, the D64_v3 offers a balanced hardware profile for legacy enterprise workloads that require high core counts and moderate memory without demanding the highest single-thread speeds. The inclusion of 1638 GB of local storage provides a cost-efficient alternative to external disk attachments for temporary data. This server is best suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure D64_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores. Operating at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 256.0 GB of RAM and 1638 GB of local storage. Benchmark data shows weak single-core performance but average multi-core scaling, with solid multi-threaded decompression and standard memory bandwidth. Lacking AVX-512 and modern GPU accelerators, it is not optimized for heavy AI inference, as reflected in poor LLM text generation metrics. However, its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial local storage make it a reliable, cost-efficient option for multi-threaded enterprise applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 1638 GB of local storage, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor on Hyper-V.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1638 GB local storage, powered by an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated virtual cores, substantial memory capacity, and integrated local storage for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4
256.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
1638 GB of integrated local storage for temporary data and caching
Average multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth suitable for standard databases
Best suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications and static web serving
